notes_rand_crosscheck: CA enacted Roberti-Roos AWB 1989; tightened 1999 (post-Stockton). Tufts marks assault==1 from 1990 onward; matches RAND.
CT 1993 AWB (post-Stockton wave); tightened post-Newtown 2013 (added more guns to list). Tufts assault==1 from 1993 onward; tightening is not a 0-to-1 transition so excluded from cohorts.
DE Lethal Weapons Act 2022. Tufts: 0->1 in 2022. Matches RAND.

HI bans assault PISTOLS only (no long guns), so Tufts coding rule (must cover long guns) yields assault==0 throughout. RAND notes HI's assault-pistol law but excludes from a state-AWB list for the same reason. No discrepancy.
IL Protect Illinois Communities Act, January 2023 (post-Highland Park). Tufts: 0->1 in 2023. Matches RAND.
MA 1998 AWB (predates federal sunset). Tufts assault==1 from 1999 onward. Already-banned at 1999, excluded from cohorts.
MD Firearm Safety Act 2013 (post-Newtown). Tufts: 0->1 in 2013. Matches RAND State Firearm Laws Database.
NJ 1990 AWB (early post-Stockton). Tufts assault==1 from 1990 onward; matches RAND.
NY enacted SAFE Act precursor in 2000; further tightened post-Newtown 2013 (NY SAFE Act). Tufts assault flips 0->1 in 2000; excluded from cohorts because 2000 - 5 = 1995 < ANALYSIS_LO.
WA HB 1240, April 2023. Tufts: 0->1 in 2023. Matches RAND.
